reason: Use the official definitive invoice rates after provincial and communal centimes: 19.25% on the 17.5% base and 9.9% on the evidence-gated Article 238 9% base.

officialSources.title: Chad 2024 Finance Law application circular
officialSources.provisions: Paragraphs 31-36 require 10% centimes on base VAT and invoices showing 19.25% and 9.9%.

officialSources.title: 2026 Finance Law application circular
officialSources.provisions: Article 229 V and paragraphs 131-133 continuing the provincial and communal centimes allocation.

failClosedRules: 9.9% is the definitive invoice rate and requires a confirmed supply listed by Article 238; it already includes centimes on the 9% base.
0% requires a confirmed Article 238 export, international transport or outbound Jet A1 case.
An Article 230 exemption is not inferred from a broad category label.
The 2026 IGL single-operation override applies strictly above XAF 50,000,000, not at equality.
The estimator flags statutory base rounding and does not silently alter entered invoice values.
No generic VAT-withholding percentage is exposed.
privacy: Calculator, invoice line and PDF remain local; the share action contains only the canonical URL.
